tagline: My Hero Academia — Mutating superpowers collide with state hero capitalism as society crumbles.
visualizationReason: MHA is governed by control webs: HPSC policy routing, inherited and stolen Quirk chains, and ideological splinter networks that determine which nodes stabilize or collapse society.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: A society built on heroes can still crack from the inside.

aiInsights.casual: My Hero Academia works like a giant social network under stress: powers keep getting stronger and stranger, while the hero system tries to keep everything marketable and under control. Every major conflict comes from that mismatch.
aiInsights.deep: The universe is a contested node-graph between biological escalation and institutional containment. Quirk singularity pressure increases variance, while HPSC ranking economics and behavioral regulation compress variance for governance. All For One and Shigaraki exploit this compression gap by converting alienated nodes into high-leverage anti-system edges, causing cascading trust and security failures.

powerSystem.name: Quirk Factor
powerSystem.subtitle: Biological Execution Layer
powerSystem.loreDesc: Quirks are inherited traits tied to a person's body and instincts, not detachable spells. Training improves expression, but the body always sets the cost.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A genetic capability stack with phenotype-specific activation paths and strict host hardware limits. Performance is bounded by physiology, recovery bandwidth, and mutation volatility.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Deku's early Full Cowl experiments show that power output without hardware adaptation causes immediate self-damage.

powerSystem.name: One For All
powerSystem.subtitle: Inherited Stockpile Network
powerSystem.loreDesc: A torch passed from wielder to wielder, carrying strength and the echoes of everyone who held it before.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A transferable accumulation engine that stores kinetic output plus vestige residues, creating a multi-tenant internal decision space that can assist or resist the current host.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Vestiges intervene during Deku's crisis points, proving OFA transfer preserves active legacy logic.

powerSystem.name: All For One
powerSystem.subtitle: Parasitic Quirk Routing
powerSystem.loreDesc: A stolen empire of powers built by taking what others were born with and redistributing it for loyalty.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A theft-and-allocation framework that centralizes Quirk ownership in a single controller node, enabling tailored loadouts while accelerating host degradation and dependence loops.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: All For One combines multiple stolen Quirks in Kamino to brute-force around single-ability counters.

powerSystem.name: State Hero Regulation
powerSystem.subtitle: Licensing and Incentive Grid
powerSystem.loreDesc: Hero work is legal only inside a managed system of schools, licenses, agencies, and rankings that sell safety to the public.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A governance layer that transforms response capacity into reputation-indexed labor markets, coupling public trust, media incentives, and deployment priorities.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Billboard rankings reshape hero behavior by tying status and opportunity to visible performance.

rankings.topTierLore: Figures like All Might and, later, Endeavor carry entire eras of public order on their shoulders.
rankings.topTierSystem: Load-bearing nodes whose failure causes broad trust and security cascades across the graph.

tiers.name: State Instruments
tiers.loreDesc: Licensed elites and covert agents who execute policy under hero branding.
tiers.systemDesc: High-authority operational nodes linked directly to HPSC control channels.

tiers.name: Mass Mobilizers
tiers.loreDesc: Ideological leaders who rally disaffected populations into anti-system blocs.
tiers.systemDesc: Narrative-amplification nodes that rapidly convert latent dissatisfaction into active edges.

tiers.name: Containment Operators
tiers.loreDesc: Teachers and field heroes who keep escalation manageable at ground level.
tiers.systemDesc: Distributed dampeners that reduce local volatility without rewriting macro incentives.

characters.name: Izuku Midoriya
characters.title: Inherited Containment Node
characters.loreBio: Born Quirkless, Deku becomes the chosen bearer of One For All and must learn to survive a power built for someone else.
characters.systemBio: A rare low-noise host selected for transfer stability; his body becomes an adaptation platform for multi-source vestige throughput.
characters.signatureMoment: Switching from bone-breaking bursts to distributed Full Cowl and auxiliary quirks to avoid hardware collapse.
characters.description: Izuku Midoriya is a  from My Hero Academia.
relationships: Boku no Hero Academia: Sukue! Kyuujo Kunren!
Boku no Hero Academia: Hero Note
Boku no Hero Academia: Training of the Dead
Boku no Hero Academia the Movie 1: Futari no Hero

characters.name: All Might
characters.title: Symbolic Load-Bearing Pillar
characters.loreBio: All Might embodies absolute reassurance, holding fear and crime down through unmatched presence.
characters.systemBio: A single deterrence super-node whose retirement exposes over-centralization risk in hero-state architecture.
characters.signatureMoment: His final stand at Kamino ends his era and removes the system's primary stabilizer.
characters.description: All Might is a  from My Hero Academia.
relationships: Boku no Hero Academia: Sukue! Kyuujo Kunren!
Boku no Hero Academia: Hero Note
Boku no Hero Academia: Training of the Dead
Boku no Hero Academia the Movie 1: Futari no Hero

characters.name: All For One
characters.title: Parasitic Routing Hub
characters.loreBio: A mythic villain who steals quirks and turns people into assets by offering power at a price.
characters.systemBio: A central extractor node that aggregates capability, manufactures dependency, and orchestrates succession through controlled heirs.
characters.signatureMoment: Engineering Shigaraki as a vessel while preserving his own continuity through transfer strategy.
characters.description: All For One is a  from My Hero Academia.
relationships: Boku no Hero Academia the Movie 1: Futari no Hero
Boku no Hero Academia the Movie 1: Futari no Hero Specials

characters.name: Tomura Shigaraki
characters.title: Entropic Singularity Node
characters.loreBio: Raised through trauma and manipulation, Shigaraki turns pure rejection into world-level destruction.
characters.systemBio: A high-variance vessel optimized to carry stacked quirks and broadcast anti-order contagion across mass networks.
characters.signatureMoment: His upgraded Decay spreads in cascading waves, converting city-scale infrastructure into instant collapse.
characters.description: Tomura Shigaraki is a  from My Hero Academia.
relationships: Boku no Hero Academia: Hero Note
Boku no Hero Academia the Movie 2: Heroes:Rising

characters.name: Enji Todoroki
characters.title: Market Apex Hero
characters.loreBio: Endeavor reaches the top through relentless force, but his family engineering project reveals the human cost of rank obsession.
characters.systemBio: An incentive-maximized node whose private eugenic optimization mirrors the public metrics logic of hero-market competition.
characters.signatureMoment: Publicly inheriting the number one position while the Dabi revelation detonates his legitimacy.
characters.description: Enji Todoroki is a  from My Hero Academia.

characters.name: Keigo Takami
characters.title: State Intelligence Instrument
characters.loreBio: Hawks is the smiling prodigy hero shaped from childhood to serve national stability at any moral cost.
characters.systemBio: A high-speed reconnaissance and covert action node embedded in both public hero branding and secret state operations.
characters.signatureMoment: Infiltrating the PLF command chain while balancing hero celebrity optics.
characters.description: Keigo Takami is a  from My Hero Academia.

characters.name: Chizome Akaguro
characters.title: Ideological Virus (Stain)
characters.loreBio: Stain hunts heroes he sees as fake, igniting a movement far larger than his own body count.
characters.systemBio: A memetic disruption node that converts isolated incidents into legitimacy crises for the state hero brand.
characters.signatureMoment: His Hosu actions trigger mass ideological recruitment among future villain cells.
characters.description: Chizome Akaguro is a  from My Hero Academia.

characters.name: Rikiya Yotsubashi
characters.title: Deregulation Architect (Re-Destro)
characters.loreBio: Re-Destro weaponizes inherited doctrine and corporate resources to demand unrestricted Quirk expression.
characters.systemBio: A capital-backed mobilization node that scales anti-regulation ideology into organized military infrastructure.
characters.signatureMoment: Merging MLA structures into the Paranormal Liberation Front under Shigaraki.
characters.description: Rikiya Yotsubashi is a  from My Hero Academia.

characters.name: Shouta Aizawa
characters.title: Quirk Dampener
characters.loreBio: Aizawa operates as a grounded teacher and field specialist who neutralizes chaos by turning powers off.
characters.systemBio: A rare suppression node that temporarily disables activation pathways, making him critical for high-threat containment operations.
characters.signatureMoment: Holding front-line control in the war despite catastrophic injury because Erasure is strategically irreplaceable.
characters.description: Shouta Aizawa is a  from My Hero Academia.
relationships: Boku no Hero Academia: Sukue! Kyuujo Kunren!
Boku no Hero Academia: Hero Note
Boku no Hero Academia: Training of the Dead
Boku no Hero Academia the Movie 2: Heroes:Rising

characters.name: Touya Todoroki
characters.title: Exposure Catalyst (Dabi)
characters.loreBio: Dabi turns personal abandonment into public vengeance, targeting both his family and hero society.
characters.systemBio: A narrative sabotage node that weaponizes leaked truth to collapse confidence in hero-market legitimacy.
characters.signatureMoment: Broadcasting his identity to fuse private abuse evidence with national legitimacy shock.
characters.description: Touya Todoroki is a  from My Hero Academia.

relationships.loreDesc: All For One raises Shigaraki as an heir while quietly preserving control over him.
relationships.systemDesc: A succession-engineering edge that seeds a future host while maintaining upstream override authority.

relationships.loreDesc: Shigaraki's rise is built on resources and conditioning supplied by his master.
relationships.systemDesc: High dependence on external provisioning for quirk augmentation and organizational scaling.

relationships.loreDesc: Midoriya inherits One For All as All Might's chosen continuation.
relationships.systemDesc: Formal transfer edge in the OFA lineage, moving system load to a new host.

relationships.loreDesc: All Might trains Deku to carry hope without collapsing under power.
relationships.systemDesc: Coaching edge that converts inherited raw throughput into survivable operational profiles.

relationships.loreDesc: Hawks gains PLF trust while secretly serving the state.
relationships.systemDesc: Deep-cover intelligence edge enabling high-value disruption of adversarial command networks.

relationships.loreDesc: Hawks works to prevent Shigaraki's coalition from reaching full activation.
relationships.systemDesc: Preemptive containment edge against singularity-class escalation.

relationships.loreDesc: Stain's anti-fake-hero ideology energizes villain recruitment that benefits Shigaraki's rise.
relationships.systemDesc: Indirect ideological feed edge that raises anti-system node throughput.

relationships.loreDesc: Stain condemns heroes motivated by status and reputation, directly clashing with Endeavor's image.
relationships.systemDesc: Normative conflict edge between purity ideology and market-optimized hero behavior.

relationships.loreDesc: Re-Destro yields command after recognizing Shigaraki's overwhelming force.
relationships.systemDesc: Authority handoff edge merging corporate militia architecture into singularity leadership.

relationships.loreDesc: Endeavor's family project leaves Touya feeling discarded, fueling Dabi's vendetta.
relationships.systemDesc: Domestic optimization failure creates a hostile leak node with privileged reputational attack vectors.

relationships.loreDesc: Dabi publicly weaponizes family truth to destroy Endeavor's symbolic authority.
relationships.systemDesc: Counter-legitimacy edge targeting a top-tier trust anchor in hero governance.

relationships.loreDesc: Aizawa pushes Midoriya toward controlled, practical Quirk usage.
relationships.systemDesc: Stability edge that lowers self-damage risk and improves containment behavior.

relationships.loreDesc: Erasure can blunt high-level Quirk activation in critical windows.
relationships.systemDesc: Direct suppression edge against catastrophic ability execution pathways.

relationships.loreDesc: Their rivalry defines the old era's central conflict between protection and domination.
relationships.systemDesc: Primary antagonistic edge between deterrence pillar and parasitic accumulation hub.

relationships.loreDesc: Both inherit legacies they did not choose, then redefine what those inheritances mean.
relationships.systemDesc: Opposed successor nodes carrying legacy stacks that compete to rewrite future system norms.

relationships.loreDesc: All For One views Hawks as a high-value state blade that must be neutralized.
relationships.systemDesc: Adversarial edge between covert state instrument and parasitic command hub.

relationships.loreDesc: Endeavor spends years chasing All Might’s unreachable standard.
relationships.systemDesc: Competitive prestige edge shaped by Billboard-era rank incentives.

factions.name: Hero Public Safety Commission
factions.loreDesc: The secretive regulator that shapes hero society through licensing, rankings, and narrative management.
factions.systemDesc: Central governance hub controlling legitimacy, resource allocation, and covert enforcement pipelines.

factions.name: Hero Agency Network
factions.loreDesc: Licensed heroes and agencies who protect civilians under public scrutiny.
factions.systemDesc: Distributed response mesh optimized for visible incident resolution and trust maintenance.

factions.name: Meta Liberation Army
factions.loreDesc: A movement that frames Quirk suppression as oppression and demands unrestricted expression.
factions.systemDesc: Ideological insurgency network converting deregulation doctrine into mass mobilization capacity.

factions.name: Paranormal Liberation Front
factions.loreDesc: A merged alliance of villains and liberation militants preparing systemic overthrow.
factions.systemDesc: Hybrid coalition that fuses nihilistic violence with scalable organizational logistics.

factions.name: League of Villains
factions.loreDesc: Shigaraki's original crew of anti-hero society actors.
factions.systemDesc: Core radicalization nucleus later expanded into the PLF's larger command network.

rules.name: Biological Hardware Limit
rules.subtitle: No body has infinite Quirk bandwidth
rules.loreConsequence: Pushing a Quirk beyond what the body can hold leads to injury, burnout, or loss of control.
rules.systemEquivalent: Host physiology enforces hard throughput ceilings; overload events produce structural damage and degraded decision quality.

rules.name: Vestige Imprint Continuity
rules.subtitle: Transferred power carries prior will
rules.loreConsequence: When inherited Quirks move hosts, traces of previous users can influence the new bearer's choices.
rules.systemEquivalent: Transferable quirks preserve legacy-state residues, creating multi-user memory interference and guidance channels.

rules.name: Quirk Singularity Escalation
rules.subtitle: Generations amplify uncontrollably
rules.loreConsequence: Each generation tends to produce harder-to-control Quirks, increasing accidents and social fear.
rules.systemEquivalent: Intergenerational mutation compounds capability complexity faster than governance and training frameworks can adapt.

rules.name: Hero Market Incentive Law
rules.subtitle: Safety is ranked and branded
rules.loreConsequence: Heroes are rewarded for visibility and public approval, which can distort priorities and behavior.
rules.systemEquivalent: Rank-indexed incentive loops couple crisis response to media-facing metrics, producing optimization drift.

rules.name: Quirk Normalization Pressure
rules.subtitle: Children are trained into acceptable expression
rules.loreConsequence: Counseling and social pressure suppress non-normative Quirk behavior, often isolating outliers.
rules.systemEquivalent: Early compliance protocols reduce short-term volatility but increase alienation among high-variance phenotypes.

rules.name: Predatory Quirk Acquisition
rules.subtitle: Stolen powers centralize control
rules.loreConsequence: All For One can remove autonomy by taking Quirks and redistributing them to create debt and obedience.
rules.systemEquivalent: Forced capability transfer enables monopoly accumulation, collapsing distributed resilience into a coercive single hub.

counterplay.mechanic: Temporarily disables activation, forcing engagements back to physical fundamentals.
counterplay.loreDesc: Aizawa levels impossible fights by turning overwhelming powers off at the right second.
counterplay.systemDesc: A suppression interrupt that cuts execution privileges before catastrophic throughput is reached.

counterplay.mechanic: Breaking contact chains and spacing nodes prevents area-wide propagation.
counterplay.loreDesc: Heroes survive by never letting Decay keep a continuous surface to travel through.
counterplay.systemDesc: Propagation denial limits cascade radius for touch-initiated chain destruction.

counterplay.mechanic: Revealed abuse and corruption puncture legitimacy and reduce compliance.
counterplay.loreDesc: Dabi and war-era disclosures shatter the polished image of hero society.
counterplay.systemDesc: Narrative integrity attacks reduce trust capital, degrading command acceptance.

counterplay.mechanic: Gradual percentage-based control avoids self-inflicted catastrophic damage.
counterplay.loreDesc: Deku survives One For All by learning to spread force instead of exploding limbs.
counterplay.systemDesc: Load-balancing strategy keeps utilization below host failure thresholds.

counterplay.mechanic: Embedded assets disrupt leadership synchronization before uprising reaches full scale.
counterplay.loreDesc: Hawks infiltrates the PLF to fracture the movement before open war.
counterplay.systemDesc: Command-disruption operation reducing adversarial network coherence.

anomalies.name: One For All
anomalies.loreDesc: A Quirk that keeps stacking power and inherited traces across generations beyond normal human design.
anomalies.systemDesc: Compounding transfer artifact that exceeds conventional host tolerances while preserving historical user state.

anomalies.name: All For One
anomalies.loreDesc: A single individual can steal and redistribute Quirks as if people were inventory.
anomalies.systemDesc: Unauthorized capability extraction protocol that bypasses inheritance boundaries and centralizes control.

anomalies.name: Nomu Bio-Weapons
anomalies.loreDesc: Engineered bodies are modified to carry multiple Quirks with minimal individuality.
anomalies.systemDesc: Artificial vessel architecture that trades cognition for stable multi-quirk load handling.

anomalies.name: Quirk-Destroying Drug
anomalies.loreDesc: A weaponized biological rewrite can erase Quirk expression itself.
anomalies.systemDesc: Targeted phenotype rollback that permanently removes activation pathways from the host.

causalEvents.name: Dawn of Quirks
causalEvents.trigger: Sudden emergence of meta abilities across the population
causalEvents.consequence: Legacy institutions fracture as society rebuilds around Quirk-bearing majorities.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The world is forced to reinvent law and daily life when powers appear everywhere.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Foundational mutation shock that resets governance assumptions and risk models.

causalEvents.name: Kamino Fracture
causalEvents.trigger: All Might and All For One's public terminal clash
causalEvents.consequence: All Might retires, exposing systemic overreliance on one symbolic enforcer.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The Symbol of Peace burns out in front of the nation.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Primary deterrence node failure causes immediate trust and crime volatility spikes.

causalEvents.name: PLF Consolidation
causalEvents.trigger: League of Villains and MLA unification under Shigaraki
causalEvents.consequence: Anti-system groups gain military scale and coherent command.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Separate villain currents fuse into one war-capable front.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Network merge event combines ideology, logistics, and destructive capability into a single threat architecture.

causalEvents.name: Paranormal Liberation War
causalEvents.trigger: State preemption collides with PLF mobilization and hidden scandals
causalEvents.consequence: Hero legitimacy and national security both degrade, pushing society toward fragmentation.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The war reveals how deep corruption and trauma run on every side.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Full-spectrum stress test where governance opacity, incentive drift, and singularity pressure converge into systemic failure.

systemQuestions.question: How does the quirk ecosystem produce both heroes and villains?
systemQuestions.answer: Quirks create asymmetric capability markets, and institutions decide which abilities are formalized, regulated, or criminalized.

systemQuestions.question: Why are rankings so central in MHA?
systemQuestions.answer: Rankings act as resource allocation and trust signals that shape patrol priority, media pressure, and recruitment flow.

systemQuestions.question: What rules govern legal force and intervention?
systemQuestions.answer: Core laws define when power use is sanctioned, which determines who can act first during crisis events.

systemQuestions.question: How do factions influence conflict outcomes?
systemQuestions.answer: Government, school, and villain networks each optimize different objectives, creating recurring systemic friction.

systemQuestions.question: How should a new reader approach this universe page?
systemQuestions.answer: Use Power Engine for capability basics, then Relationship Matrix to understand mentorship, rivalry, and succession pressure.

hero.microHook: Track control edges before reading motives.
hero.thesis: MHA is governed by control webs: HPSC policy routing, inherited and stolen Quirk chains, and ideological splinter networks that determine w…

introductionSummary: My Hero Academia maps a society where Quirk inheritance chains, hero school institutions, and villain syndicates each form distinct control networks — and All For One and One For All represent the system's two poles of power consolidation versus power redistribution. The node graph captures what drives the narrative: not individual battles, but which institutional and Quirk dependency networks survive the collapse of hero capitalism.
